topic: Post-patch re-review of WIN-062 Tesla longitudinal wave 1.574c after decider applied 'third->one-of-at-least-three' factual fix (queue_id 184)
change_summary: Decider's a0f217d commit applied the prior batch review's hole #3 recommendation: 'three parallel acknowledge-without-fix moves' was hedged to 'one of at least three parallel acknowledge-without-fix moves' in the EXP-181 pattern paragraph. This acknowledges that ai_manifest.json registry_semantics (chg-20260417-1620-005) is a fourth parallel self-caveat from the same 24-hour window, without yet naming it inline. detail_evidence_length 5143 -> 5156 (+13 chars).
our_argument_summary: WIN-062 (verdict: Misleading) argues Tesla's US Patent 787412 depicts a spherical Earth, his ~1.57c value is a known Earth-ionosphere waveguide phase velocity on a sphere, and the dome's disc diameter (40,030 km) was fitted to match the globe's circumference (40,075 km) within 0.11%, producing numerical agreement by construction. The detail_evidence appends an EXP-181 registry-integrity-notes block crediting the dome author's 2026-04-17 machine-readable disambiguation (claim_key win-062-tesla-wave-speed collapses two visible WIN-062 blocks to one slug) while showing the visible-prose duplicate persists and both CONFIRMED badges still contribute to the 69-tally. The patched sentence now reads 'this is one of at least three parallel acknowledge-without-fix moves shipped inside a 24-hour window on 2026-04-17.'

relevant_files: monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/WIN-058-062-post-EXP-181.json (prior batch review, hole #3 = the patch trigger)
monitor/analyst/expansions/EXP-181.json (three-patch package that first landed the pattern paragraph)
monitor/changes/chg-20260417-1620-005.json (ai_manifest.json registry_semantics, the unnamed fourth instance the hedge now acknowledges)
git log a0f217d (decider commit applying the patch)
dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: No dome-side monitor.py domain for WIN-062 Tesla-wave-speed. No API/fetch. The 1.574c number is stated in the dome's WIN list and derived from disc_diameter * 2 / 0.08484 s (round-trip time quoted from Tesla's 1899 Colorado Springs work). The 'monitoring: none' tag remains correct — verified against repo search for 'tesla', '1.574', '471240', '0.08484' which returns no monitor.py hit.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: N/A for the Tesla value itself — no monitor.py 'check' exists. The dome treats Tesla's 1899 published velocity as the 'observation' and labels it CONFIRMED in prose. The patch itself is a one-phrase edit; no code or data pipeline involvement.
data_sources_used: Tesla US Patent 787412 (1905) — for the 1.574c claim
Earth-ionosphere waveguide literature (DOI 10.1029/JZ071i004p01171) — for the kernel-of-truth that ~1.57c phase velocity is genuine physics on a sphere
dome repository scripts test_curve_stretching.py, find_curve.py (per WIN-062.c2 citation check, verified as present in dome repo) — for the WGS84-fitting argument
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: 'At least three' is itself a hedge — it concedes that more instances may exist without committing to a specific count. This is defensive writing. The three explicitly named instances (methodology.json timestamp_scope, methodology.json registry_counts, claim_index.json registry_integrity_notes) all live in commit 6e06efb (2026-04-17). The ai_manifest.json registry_semantics block (chg-005) lives in commits 6e06efb,5021eec,a57e731 — overlapping commit but different layer (AI-manifest vs claim-registry). The hedge is honest; the fourth instance is discoverable in our own change files but is not named in the WIN-062 prose itself.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: The patch is a pure prose edit — no code/data claims changed. The pre-existing code_analysis tags (monitoring=none, relabels_standard=true, post_hoc=true, derives_from_dome=false) are unchanged and remain correct.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: Fresh repo-wide grep for 'tesla', '1.574', '471240', '40030', '0.08484' returns no monitor.py references. The dome has no Tesla-wave-speed domain or data-fetch logic. The WIN appears in the catalog and is labeled CONFIRMED based on Tesla's 1899 measurement, not any automated check. WIN-062.c2 tagged 'none'; WIN-058-062-post-EXP-181 confirmed 'none'. Unchanged by this patch.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: ~1.57c is a published phase-velocity figure for ELF/VLF waves in the spherical Earth-ionosphere waveguide (Galejs 1966, DOI 10.1029/JZ071i004p01171, citation verified in WIN-062.c2). The dome keeps the same numerical value but relabels the propagation medium from 'spherical waveguide' to 'flat-disc aether'. No new value; only the cause is renamed.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Tesla's Colorado Springs measurements date to 1899 and were published in US Patent 787412 in 1905 and in his 'Colorado Springs Notes' (Nikola Tesla Museum edition, Beograd 1978). The dome's adoption of 1.574c as a 'prediction' long post-dates public availability of the value. No pre-registration in any independent timestamping venue exists for the dome's 1.574c claim.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: The disc_diameter (40,030 km) was fitted to globe circumference (40,075 km, WGS84-derived) by dome repository scripts test_curve_stretching.py and find_curve.py (per WIN-062.c2 citation check). The 1.574c value is then produced by dividing that fitted disc_diameter by Tesla's stated round-trip time — so the chain is globe-circumference -> fitted disc_diameter -> dome 'prediction'. No dome-geometry-first derivation path.

holes_found.description: Post-hoc verdict boilerplate mismatches monitoring=none. detail_verdict_text says: 'the monitor script hardcodes the target number on the same commit as the data-fetch line, so the temporal ordering the definition requires cannot be checked from the repository history.' But this WIN has monitoring=none — there IS no monitor.py entry for Tesla-wave-speed, no data-fetch line, no target-number hardcode line. A dome defender can legitimately reply: 'Your own code_analysis says there is no monitor.py entry for this WIN. Your post-hoc argument about the monitor script hardcoding the target on the data-fetch commit therefore does not apply here.' The boilerplate was written for the hardcoded-domain WINs and is carelessly reused here. The underlying post-hoc critique IS valid (Tesla's 1899 value was public knowledge when the dome adopted it), but the reasoning needs to be reframed for a no-monitor WIN: the claim is post-hoc because the 1.574c figure lives in the dome's prose catalog, adopted verbatim from a 1905 patent, with no pre-registration of the expected value in any independent timestamping venue before the dome published its match. That framing is honest for monitoring=none WINs; the monitor-script framing is not.
holes_found.recommendation: Rewrite the second paragraph of detail_verdict_text to match monitoring=none: 'Independent of the scientific critique above, this claim also fails our post-hoc test. The 1.574c figure was published by Tesla in 1905 (US Patent 787412) and restated in his Colorado Springs Notes (Nikola Tesla Museum, Beograd 1978); the value has been in the scientific record for over a century. The dome catalog adopts this published number verbatim and labels it CONFIRMED, without pre-registering the expected value in any timestamping venue independent of the author before the match was claimed. No monitor.py automation runs for this WIN (monitoring: none), so the repository-history test the dome author's definition requires is inapplicable here — but the post-hoc structure is at the prose-catalog level and is unaffected by the absence of automation.' This keeps the post-hoc verdict intact while fixing the mismatched framing.

holes_found.description: The 'at least three' hedge names three instances but leaves the fourth unnamed inline, which is a readable opening for a careful dome defender. Block structure: 'Taken together with [timestamp_scope] and [registry_counts], this is one of at least three parallel acknowledge-without-fix moves shipped inside a 24-hour window on 2026-04-17.' A defender reading this counts exactly three named instances (timestamp_scope, registry_counts, claim_index.json registry_integrity_notes) and then sees 'at least three' — a hedge that implies more without naming any. Defender response: 'What is the fourth? If you have it, cite it. If you don't, why hedge?' The answer is ai_manifest.json registry_semantics (chg-005), which our own change files document. The prior batch review's hole #3 offered two fixes: (a) add a parenthetical naming the fourth instance, (b) keep the hedge and defer to ISS-1087. The decider chose (b). That is defensible — ISS-1087 may propagate the full enumeration later — but the WIN-062 prose itself is the highest-traffic place this pattern is cataloged in user-visible text, and a reader rarely follows a hedge-referenced open issue. Advocate-mode defender would hammer this as 'the hedge covers a miscount the authors already know about.'
holes_found.recommendation: Consider option (a) from the prior review in a later touch: append a short parenthetical in WIN-062 (mirrored in WIN-058 and WIN-058b) of the form '(ai_manifest.json registry_semantics, see ISS-1087, is a fourth parallel instance in the same window)'. Not urgent — the current hedge is honest — but the parenthetical would close the rhetorical opening. Track as a follow-up, not a blocker. If ISS-1087's eventual resolution does NOT backfill the enumeration here, revisit.

holes_found.description: Commit attribution remains inconsistent across the three EXP-181-touched WIN blocks. WIN-058 says 'three instances in one commit (6e06efb, 2026-04-17)'. WIN-058b says 'Three documentary concessions in a single 24-hour window'. WIN-062 now says 'one of at least three parallel acknowledge-without-fix moves shipped inside a 24-hour window on 2026-04-17'. Prior batch review #5 recommended standardizing to 'three instances in commit 6e06efb (2026-04-17)'. The 'at least three' patch moved WIN-062 further from this standardization rather than toward it — the hedge is arguably incompatible with the narrow 'one commit' framing because the hedge invites external instances in the same window. A careful reader comparing the three blocks sees three different levels of precision. Low-severity imprecision rather than error.
holes_found.recommendation: Either standardize on 'in commit 6e06efb (2026-04-17), with at least one adjacent-commit instance (ai_manifest.json registry_semantics)' OR accept that the hedge-with-window is now the canonical framing for WIN-062 and nudge WIN-058 / WIN-058b to match the hedge. Pick one framing and apply uniformly — mixing them is the hole. No urgency; batch this with a future EXP-181 touch-up or an ISS-1087 resolution.

holes_found.description: onclick="showTab('timestamp');return false" UX issue persists on the Timestamp Error cross-reference anchor. The return-false cancels the default anchor jump to id="timestamp-error", so after the tab switch the reader lands at the top of the Timestamp Error tab rather than at the specific April-2026 update block the link targets. Not a broken link (tab switch works, anchor id exists), but a worse-than-possible landing. Same pattern as flagged in WIN-058-062-post-EXP-181 hole #6; unchanged by the current patch.
holes_found.recommendation: Refactor to onclick="showTab('timestamp')" (drop the 'return false') so the default anchor jump to id="timestamp-error" runs after the tab switch. Verify the jump lands inside the April-2026 update block in the switched-tab DOM. Lowest priority — cosmetic UX, consistent with WIN-058/058b treatment.

holes_found.description: Velocity-number precision mismatch persists (previously flagged in WIN-062.c2 hole #3 and unaddressed). Text says 'Tesla states a velocity of 471,240 km/s (~1.57c) for a 0.08484s round-trip.' 471,240 / 299,792.458 = 1.5719c, which the dome rounds up to 1.574c. The dome's '1.574c' corresponds to 471,874 km/s, a 634 km/s gap from our stated Tesla figure. A forensic defender can say 'You cite 471,240 km/s for Tesla and 1.574c for the dome; these are not the same number.' The review text already discloses the 0.24% rounding mismatch in its next sentence, which partially mitigates this. Still, the claim title 'Tesla longitudinal wave 1.574c' plus the detail '471,240 km/s' needs a one-sentence acknowledgement that the dome's 1.574c is itself a rounded-up restatement of Tesla's 1.5719c.
holes_found.recommendation: Insert after 'Tesla states a velocity of 471,240 km/s (~1.57c)': 'The dome's 1.574c headline slightly rounds 1.5719c up; the dome is using a restated approximation of Tesla's original figure.' Carries no verdict weight — a cosmetic tightening. Batch with any future WIN-062 touch.

stronger_arguments.description: Credit the patch explicitly in the Part 2b aggregate write-up. The decider's application of hole #3 (with the softer option (b)) is itself a small proof-point that our adversarial-review pipeline is tightening WIN prose post-integration: the batch review flagged a rhetorical undercount, the decider shipped a hedge, and a human reader can now see both a credit-where-due block and a self-acknowledged scope hedge inside the same WIN paragraph. Useful as a template citation when we describe our own review methodology in contrast to the dome's acknowledge-without-fix pattern.

Advocate-mode defender reading: 'The patch concedes your own undercount. The dome author's three named concessions plus my fourth (registry_semantics) is now, by your own hedge, at least four acknowledgements in a single day. You are describing a standard methodology, not an isolated lapse — which means the author is working, not stonewalling.' This is the strongest dome-defender rejoinder. Counter: acknowledgement-without-fix is still acknowledgement-without-fix; the cumulative count of parallel self-caveats raises rather than lowers the methodology concern (why are four layers flagged as needing caveats on one day?). Adding this rebuttal prophylactically to the WIN-062 block (or to SEC-8.17) would blunt the advocate read.

kernel_of_truth.description: The patched sentence correctly concedes our prior under-count. 'One of at least three' is honest; the dome author DID ship multiple parallel self-caveats on 2026-04-17 and our previous 'three' formulation silently excluded the ai_manifest.json registry_semantics block. The decider's decision to hedge rather than enumerate is a reasonable compromise given (a) the parenthetical-option would have expanded the paragraph further, (b) ISS-1087 is the designated place to catalog the full registry-caveat stack. The hedge preserves the pattern paragraph's rhetorical force while removing the numerical overclaim.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: The patch is corrective rather than substantive — it fixes a counting error in our argument, not a problem with the dome's claim. WIN-062's verdict (Misleading) still rests on (a) Tesla's patent drawings depict a spherical Earth, (b) ~1.57c is a known waveguide phase velocity on a sphere, (c) the dome's disc_diameter was fitted to globe circumference via WGS84, making the numerical match a construction artifact. None of that depends on the count. The patch improves our rhetorical precision without changing the scientific verdict.

cross_win_consistency.verdict_stability: Verdict remains 'Misleading'. Unchanged from c1 and c2.
cross_win_consistency.code_analysis_tag_stability: All four tags unchanged (monitoring=none, relabels_standard=true, post_hoc=true, derives_from_dome=false). Consistent with WIN-062.c2, WIN-058-062-post-EXP-181, and current wins.json.
cross_win_consistency.sibling_block_consistency: The 'at least three' hedge is currently unique to WIN-062; WIN-058 still says 'three instances in one commit' and WIN-058b still says 'Three documentary concessions in a single 24-hour window.' Sibling blocks have drifted further apart after this patch, not closer together. See hole #3 above. Recommended future patch: propagate the hedge (or enumerate the fourth) uniformly across all three.
cross_win_consistency.sec_2b_aggregate_consistency: monitoring=none here; no aggregate tally impact since Part 2b's hardcoded/live-fetch/none breakdown was already treating WIN-062 as none.

deeper_analysis: The patch is small, targeted, and correctly-applied. It addresses the prior batch review's moderate hole #3 (undercount of parallel acknowledge-without-fix moves) by switching from a flat 'three' to a hedged 'at least three.' This is the softer of the two options the prior review offered; it preserves the pattern paragraph's rhetorical force while removing the specific counting error. The verdict (Misleading) holds without dependency on the count, so the patch does not perturb the scientific argument. What the patch does NOT fix: the hedge names no fourth instance inline, which creates a minor rhetorical opening a dome defender can press ('what is the fourth?'). The ai_manifest.json registry_semantics block (chg-005) is the unnamed instance; ISS-1087 is the designated follow-up for the full enumeration. Separately, I surface one moderate issue the patch did not touch and that no prior review appears to have flagged specifically for WIN-062: the post-hoc boilerplate in detail_verdict_text refers to 'the monitor script hardcodes the target number on the same commit as the data-fetch line.' WIN-062 has monitoring=none — there is no monitor.py entry for Tesla-wave-speed, so the monitor-script framing is inapplicable. The post-hoc verdict remains correct on substance (Tesla's 1899 value was public knowledge when the dome adopted it and there is no independent pre-registration), but the framing is mismatched and a careful defender can use the mismatch to cast doubt on our rigor. Recommended action is minor_edit to reframe that paragraph for monitoring=none WINs. Advocate-mode check: a strong dome defender's best reading of the patched block is 'the authors conceded their own undercount, which proves they are reviewing rigorously.' That is a fair reading, and our ISS-1087 follow-up is the place to close the loop. No new critical or major issues introduced by the patch.

DOI 10.1029/JZ071i004p01171 — Earth-ionosphere waveguide citation, verified as Galejs (1966) in WIN-062.c2, unchanged
test_curve_stretching.py, find_curve.py — verified as dome repository scripts in WIN-062.c2, unchanged
Section 6.2 cross-reference via '#part6' with showTab('predictions') — verified correct for V6 layout in WIN-062.c2, unchanged
commit 6e06efb as home of the three named instances — verified in WIN-058-062-post-EXP-181 code_analysis, unchanged
chg-20260417-1620-005.json as home of the unnamed fourth instance (ai_manifest.json registry_semantics) — verified in WIN-058-062-post-EXP-181 holes_found #3 and in this review
Patch application on git commit a0f217d with commit message 'Part 4.6 renumber, WIN-058/058b/062 factual fixes' — verified via git log in clone
citations_failed: Post-hoc verdict boilerplate referencing 'monitor script hardcodes the target number on the same commit as the data-fetch line' is a citation-like structural claim that does not apply to WIN-062 (monitoring=none). Flagged as moderate hole #1 above.
citations_unchecked: Whether the live 2026-04-19 wins.html still renders two separate WIN-062 CONFIRMED blocks (no live fetch performed in this review; last confirmed present 2026-04-18 per prior batch review)
Whether claim_key 'win-062-tesla-wave-speed' in claim_index.json has been touched since the 2026-04-17 introduction (no re-fetch this pass)

advocate_mode_reading.strongest_defender_argument: The hedge 'at least three' concedes your own undercount; the dome author is shipping multiple synchronized self-caveats; that is not stonewalling, it is methodology tightening. Your review is a feedback loop and the dome site is responding constructively. You should credit that as progress, not pattern.
advocate_mode_reading.our_response: Acknowledgement without fix is still acknowledgement without fix. Four parallel self-caveats on the machine layer in a single 24-hour window, with none of them touching the visible-prose registry or the 69-confirmed headline count, is a pattern of AI-auditor-layer repair with human-reader-layer decay. Progress on the AI-facing audit path does not restore the prose catalog a human reader sees. The patch correctly acknowledges our counting error; it does not refute the structural critique.
